{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}}''|前方後方墳}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]

The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
