{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]

Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Nagano (city)]] [[Category:History of Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11 4]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:imported Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun]] [[ja:川柳将軍塚古墳]] [[ja:川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳]] [[sn:Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=川柳将軍塚古墳| 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=長野市}}| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|34|11.298|N|138|6|34.042|E|}}| 所在地=長野県長野市篠ノ井石川| 形状=前方後円墳| 埋葬施設=竪穴式石室| 規模=全長91m| 築造年代=4世紀前半| 出土品=円筒埴輪、埴輪棺、装身具、銅鏡、銅鏃、石製品、筒型銅器| 史跡指定=[[昭和]]52年7月14日 国指定| }} '''川柳将軍塚古墳'''（せんりゅうしょうぐんづかこふん）は、[[長野県]][[長野市]]にある[[前方後円墳]]。[[長野盆地|善光寺平]]では[[森将軍塚古墳]]に次ぐ規模を誇る。名称は[[更級郡]][[川柳村 (長野県)|川柳村]]にちなむ。
[[1977年]][[7月14日]]に近辺の姫塚古墳とともに国の史跡に指定された。登録名称は「'''川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳'''」<ref name=bunka>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp 国指定文化財等データベース]</ref>。
== 概要 == 川柳将軍塚古墳は、[[長野盆地|善光寺平]]の西辺を画する断層崖上に築かれた前方後円墳で、[[1927年]]、川柳将軍塚保存会によって考古学者[[森本六爾]]の指導で発掘調査が実施された<ref name=chikuma>『千曲市森将軍塚古墳館ガイドブック』34頁</ref>。全長は約91メートル、後円部径42メートル、前方部幅31メートルをはかり、前方部を北北東に向ける<ref name=bunka>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp 国指定文化財等データベース]</ref>。くびれ部の西側には部分的な濠の痕跡かと思われる地形が残る。
後円部と前方部の頂部には[[竪穴式石室]]と推定される主体部がともに主軸に平行して配置されていたと伝えられる。[[享保]]年間や[[寛政]]年間（1799年）に盗掘をうけ、多数の鏡や碧玉製車輪石などが出土した（[[松代藩]]が預かったが[[明治]]の廃藩で[[布施神社]]の宝物とされた）ことが記録（[[信濃奇勝録]]等）されているが、現存するものは、倣製[[内行花文鏡]]をはじめとする鏡8面、碧玉製垂飾、[[勾玉]]、管玉、切子玉、小玉などである<ref name=bunka>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp 国指定文化財等データベース]</ref>。付近から発掘された埴輪棺の特長から[[千曲川]]対岸の[[森将軍塚古墳]]に続く4世紀代の古墳であると思われ<ref name=chikuma>『千曲市森将軍塚古墳館ガイドブック』34頁</ref>、豊富な副葬品をもった善光寺平最古期の古墳として重要である<ref name=bunka>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp 国指定文化財等データベース]</ref>。
== 姫塚古墳 == 将軍塚古墳の北方200mに位置する[[前方後方墳]]。全長31mで未調査のため詳細不明。前方後方墳は善光寺平では稀少であり、将軍塚古墳との前後関係が重要な問題となる<ref>「日本古墳大辞典」487－488頁</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[中部地方の史跡一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1016.html 長野市文化財データベース]
{{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:せんりゆうしようぐんつかこふん}} [[Category:長野県の古墳]] [[Category:長野市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:長野県にある国指定の史跡]]

[[1977年]][[7月14日]]に近辺の姫塚古墳とともに国の史跡に指定された。登録名称は「'''川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳'''」<ref name=bunka>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp 国指定文化財等データベース]</ref>。
== 概要 == 川柳将軍塚古墳は、[[長野盆地|善光寺平]]の西辺を画する断層崖上に築かれた前方後円墳で、[[1927年]]、川柳将軍塚保存会によって考古学者[[森本六爾]]の指導で発掘調査が実施された<ref name=chikuma>『千曲市森将軍塚古墳館ガイドブック』34頁</ref>。全長は約91メートル、後円部径42メートル、前方部幅31メートルをはかり、前方部を北北東に向ける<ref name=bunka>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp 国指定文化財等データベース]</ref>。くびれ部の西側には部分的な濠の痕跡かと思われる地形が残る。
後円部と前方部の頂部には[[竪穴式石室]]と推定される主体部がともに主軸に平行して配置されていたと伝えられる。[[享保]]年間や[[寛政]]年間（1799年）に盗掘をうけ、多数の鏡や碧玉製車輪石などが出土した（[[松代藩]]が預かったが[[明治]]の廃藩で[[布施神社]]の宝物とされた）ことが記録（[[信濃奇勝録]]等）されているが、現存するものは、倣製[[内行花文鏡]]をはじめとする鏡8面、碧玉製垂飾、[[勾玉]]、管玉、切子玉、小玉などである<ref name=bunka>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp 国指定文化財等データベース]</ref>。付近から発掘された埴輪棺の特長から[[千曲川]]対岸の[[森将軍塚古墳]]に続く4世紀代の古墳であると思われ<ref name=chikuma>『千曲市森将軍塚古墳館ガイドブック』34頁</ref>、豊富な副葬品をもった善光寺平最古期の古墳として重要である<ref name=bunka>[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/searchlist.asp 国指定文化財等データベース]</ref>。
== 姫塚古墳 == 将軍塚古墳の北方200mに位置する[[前方後方墳]]。全長31mで未調査のため詳細不明。前方後方墳は善光寺平では稀少であり、将軍塚古墳との前後関係が重要な問題となる<ref>「日本古墳大辞典」487－488頁</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[中部地方の史跡一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1016.html 長野市文化財データベース]

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1016.html Nagano city cultural database] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.go-nagano.net/topics_detail6/id=808 Nagao Prefecture Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}}

It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. Both chambers were lined with split [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[Japan]] | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] [[Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[Nagano, Nagano]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]

The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano, Nagano]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano, Nagano]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[:en:Paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[:en:Western Han|Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[Shinonoi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Shinonoi Station|Shinonoi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Shin'etsu Main Line|Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Pair of Kofun period tumuli in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|34|11|N|138|06|34|E|region:JP-16|display=title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano, Nagano]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[:en:Paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[:en:Western Han|Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Shinonoi Station|Shinonoi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Shin'etsu Main Line|Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nagano)]]

As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Shinonoi Station|Shinonoi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Shin'etsu Main Line|Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Himezuka Kofun | native_name = 川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | alternate_name = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.56972222|N|138.10944444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Nagano Prefecture#Japan | relief = 1 | map_alt = Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun | map_caption = location in Japan | map_size = 270px | location = [[:en:Nagano, Nagano|Nagano, Nagano]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] | type = kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = ruins | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun - Himezuka Kofun'''|川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳||}} is a pair of [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|tumuli]], located in the Shinonoi-ishikawa neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano]] in the [[:en:Chubu region|Chubu region]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. They have has been protected under a single [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]] designation since 1977.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/216469 |title=川柳将軍塚古墳・姫塚古墳 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2017}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==Overview== The '''Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun''' is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the western slope of Mount Shimomoi at the western edge of the Nagano Basin, with the ruins of a small settlement and [[:en:Paddy field|rice fields]] at its base. The size of the mound is 93 meters in length, with a 45-meter diameter posterior circular portion, ten meters in height and a 26 meter long anterior rectangular portion, five meters in height, and is orientated north-northeast. It was once covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' and cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and had an encircling moat, small traces of which still exist. The tumulus had two vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s arranged parallel to the main axis, one on each portion of the mound. Both chambers were lined with split [[:en:Andesite|Andesite]] stone blocks, and were painted in [[:en:Vermillion|Vermillion]]. The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide. The anterior chamber was 2.7 meters long by 1.2 meters wide. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tomb was robbed by local farmers in 1800, who found that it contained a large number of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]], including 42 [[Shinju-kyo|bronze mirror]]s, wheel-like stones, copper tubes, jewelry and beads, ''[[magatama]]'', and fragments of armor. The site was re-[[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1927. The number of bronze mirrors from this tumulus was extraordinarily large, but the whereabouts of only six are known today. Of these six, one had been determined to have been of [[:en:Western Han|Western Han]] provenience, and the others were made in Japan. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The style of the ''haniwa'' recovered at the site is very similar to that of the [[Hanishina Kofun group#Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun|Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun]], leading archaeologists to concur that this tomb was built from the end of the 4th century to the first half of the 5th century. As with the Mori-Shōgunzuka Kofun, it is regarded as a tomb of the local king with close relations to the [[:en:Imperial House of Japan|imperial dynasty]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The '''Himezuka Kofun''' is a "two conjoined rectangles" typed {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and is located 200 meter north of the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun on the same ridge. Its total length is 31 meters. The rear portion is 20 meters high with a height of four meters and the front portion has height of two meters. It has not been excavated, but from its style is thought to date to around the middle of the 4th century, and to thus pre-date the Senryū-Shōgunzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is approximately ten minutes by car from [[:en:Shinonoi Station|Shinonoi Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Shin'etsu Main Line|Shin'etsu Main Line]]; however, there are no public facilities and the tombs are located in an area with dense forests.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The chamber in the posterior domed-portion was 10.3 meters in length by 1.8 meters wide.
